app.name: PowerLab
auth.signInDesc: Sign in with your computer username and password
auth.checkGateway: Check that the gateway is running, then try again
setup.welcomeDesc: Your high-performance control center is ready. Let's set up your master account to get started.
setup.note: Note: Currently, this account is restricted to the PowerLab panel and does not provision a user on the host OS.
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.diskTemperatureTooltip: Current disk temperature reported by SMART
dashboard.smartStatusTooltip: Overall SMART self-test result from smartctl
dashboard.checkBackend: Check that the backend is running at the configured address.
files.title: File Manager
files.fileTooLargeForEditor: File is too large to open in the editor (>10 MB). Use right-click → Download instead.
files.deleteItemsPrompt: Delete {count} items? This action cannot be undone.
files.deleteItemPrompt: Delete this item? This action cannot be undone.
pwa.installDesc: Add to your home screen for the full experience
settings.mdnsDesc: Used as the mDNS name. Reachable at {url}
settings.hostnamePlanned: Hostname configuration via UI is planned. For now, edit at the OS level.
settings.networkDesc: How devices on your local network reach PowerLab.
settings.httpsWarning: HTTPS via self-signed cert is disabled by default. Browsers will warn about HTTP. For warning-free HTTPS, install a local CA via mkcert.
settings.listenPortDesc: The HTTP port the gateway binds. Currently {port}.
settings.portWarning: Changing the port disconnects this session for ~3 seconds while the gateway re-binds. We'll redirect you automatically.
settings.appsDesc: Where app data lives and where store apps come from.
settings.storagePathDesc: Bind-mount root for installed apps. Volume sources prefixed with /DATA are remapped here.
settings.storageConfigDesc: Configured at startup via app-management.conf. UI editing planned.
settings.sourcesConfigDesc: Edit sources via app-management.conf. UI editing planned.
settings.verificationDesc: Test if your device trusts PowerLab.
settings.bcryptNotice: PowerLab uses a bcrypt-hashed local user. OS-level authentication (PAM/dscl) is on the roadmap.
settings.aboutPanelDesc: The headless OS panel for home servers and edge boxes. Lightning-fast, minimal, and built to run flawlessly on hardware everyone else gave up on.
settings.updatesDesc: Checks the PowerLab GitHub release manifest hourly.
settings.tooOldError: Cannot upgrade directly from {current} to {available}. Upgrade to an intermediate release first.
settings.skippedError: The maintainer pulled {version} after publishing it. Wait for the next release.
settings.noArchError: {version} does not ship a build for this architecture.
settings.checkManifestHint: Click "Check now" to fetch the latest manifest.
settings.trustEstablished: Trust established! Redirecting to secure connection…
settings.trustFailed: Connection failed. Please ensure the certificate is installed and trusted.
settings.portLimitError: Port must be an integer between 1 and 65535
settings.alreadyCurrentPort: That is already the current port
settings.confirmSignOut: Are you sure you want to sign out?
apps.updatePrompt: A new version of {title} is ready to be installed.
apps.startContainerToSeeLogs: Start the container to see its real-time output here.
apps.emptyServerDesc: Your server is empty. Explore the App Store to install your first container.
form.webPortDesc: The external port used to access this application's web interface.
apps.errorLinuxOnly: This app needs Linux-only kernel devices (e.g. /dev/net/tun for VPN apps) that aren't available on this system.
apps.errorArch: This app is not available for your CPU architecture.
apps.errorPull: The Docker image could not be pulled. It may have been removed or is no longer public.
apps.errorPortAlloc: Another service is using a port this app needs. Try restarting and reinstalling.
apps.errorManifest: This Compose file is missing the x-powerlab (or x-casaos) manifest required by PowerLab.
apps.errorAlreadyInstalling: This app is already installing. Wait for it to finish before trying again.
apps.errorBackend: Could not reach the backend. Check that the PowerLab service is running.
apps.errorSubnetExhausted: Docker has run out of network subnets. The fix is below — clean orphaned networks, then retry.
apps.subnetRemediationExplain: Docker keeps a finite pool of subnets for compose networks. Run these on the server (SSH or terminal) to free up unused ones — they are safe; only orphaned containers and networks are removed.
apps.subnetRemediationFollowup: After running both, click Retry. If it still fails, the pool needs to be expanded in /etc/docker/daemon.json — see the docs.
apps.pullingImage: Pulling Docker image and starting containers
apps.appRunningDesc: App is running and available on your Launchpad.
apps.installInBackground: The install is still running in the background. Check your Launchpad in a few minutes.
apps.portsFreeDesc: All chosen ports are free. Install will use these.
apps.editPortsDesc: Edit the highlighted ports or click Auto to pick the next free one.
apps.forkOriginalUntouched: Your edits land in a separate Custom App. The store version of this app is never modified.
apps.forkUniqueName: Give it a unique name in the editor before deploying.
apps.removeAppDesc: Remove this app and its containers?
apps.deleteAppDataDesc: This will permanently remove all configuration and files.
launchpad.interactionsHint: Click to open · Drag to reorder · Hold to manage
launchpad.forkDesc: You can modify the Compose YAML and configuration. A new app will be created, and the store version will stay installed.
error.unauthorized: Session expired. Please log in again.
error.passTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
error.passTooShortBackend: Password is too short — minimum 8 characters
error.setupFailed: Failed to initialize the system. Check the backend logs.
error.setupKeyExpired: Setup session expired. Reload the page to start over.
error.userExists: That username is already taken — choose another.
settings.apiDocsDesc: Explore v1 and v2 endpoints with Scalar
apps.uninstallDataPreserved: Your data and config files in /DATA/AppData are preserved.
settings.trustEstablishedRedirect: Trust established! Redirecting to secure connection…
settings.alreadySecure: Already on the secure URL — your trust is verified. ✓
settings.trustResetSuccess: Trust reset. Re-run "Test Connection" when ready.
settings.caRotatedSuccess: CA rotated. Re-install the new CA on every device that needs to reach the panel.
settings.portRangeError: Port must be an integer between 1 and 65535
settings.caKeepHint: Easiest fix: click "Keep" on Chrome's warning
settings.caKeepExplain: Chrome flags the certificate file as a high-risk download because the page isn't trusted yet (chicken-and-egg). Clicking "Keep" on the toolbar warning is safe — the file is your own server's certificate.
settings.caBlockedExplain: If "Keep" isn't available on your browser, open the download in a plain HTTP tab — Chrome's high-risk-file rule fires only on HTTPS-untrusted contexts.
settings.caOpenViaHttpHint: Open the download in a new HTTP tab. Bypasses Chrome's HTTPS-context download blocker.
form.nameInvalidChars: Use lowercase letters, digits, hyphen, underscore, or dot
